docs.title: FinWise Python SDK
docs.text:
Unofficial Python SDK for the FinWise API

Unofficial SDK

This is an unofficial, community-maintained Python SDK for the FinWise API. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or officially supported by FinWise.

A simple, type-safe Python client for the FinWise API.

For the official API documentation, see finwiseapp.io/docs/api.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Type-safe: Full type hints and Pydantic models
  • Automatic retries: Exponential backoff for transient errors
  • Simple API: Returns plain Python lists for easy iteration
  • Context manager: Automatic resource cleanup

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
from finwise import FinWise

client = FinWise(api_key="your-api-key")

# List all accounts
accounts = client.accounts.list()
for account in accounts:
    print(f"{account.name}: {account.currency} {account.balance}")

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.9+
  • httpx
  • pydantic

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT License - see LICENSE for details.

docs.title: Client Options
docs.text:
from finwise import FinWise

client = FinWise(
    api_key="your-api-key",
    base_url="https://api.finwiseapp.io",  # Default
    timeout=30.0,    # Request timeout in seconds
    max_retries=3,   # Automatic retries for transient errors
)
Parameter Type Default Description api_key str None Your FinWise API key. Falls back to FINWISE_API_KEY env var base_url str https://api.finwiseapp.io API base URL timeout float 30.0 Request timeout in seconds max_retries int 3 Number of retries for transient errors

docs.title: Environment Variable
docs.text:
Set your API key via environment variable to avoid hardcoding it:

export FINWISE_API_KEY="your-api-key"

from finwise import FinWise

# Automatically uses FINWISE_API_KEY
client = FinWise()

docs.title: Context Manager
docs.text:
Use the client as a context manager for automatic cleanup:

from finwise import FinWise

with FinWise(api_key="your-api-key") as client:
    accounts = client.accounts.list()
    # Client is automatically closed when exiting the block

This ensures the underlying HTTP connection is properly closed when you're done.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: • Python 3.11 or higher

docs.title: From PyPI
docs.text:
Install the SDK using pip:

pip install finwise-python

docs.title: Using Nix
docs.text:
If you have Nix installed with flakes enabled, you can use this package directly:

# Build the package
nix build github:rameezk/finwise-python

# Or use in your flake.nix
{
  inputs.finwise-python.url = "github:rameezk/finwise-python";
}

docs.title: For Development
docs.text:
If you want to contribute or modify the SDK:

pip install finwise-python[dev]

Or with Nix:

git clone https://github.com/rameezk/finwise-python.git
cd finwise-python
nix develop
pip install -e .

docs.title: For Documentation
docs.text:
To build the documentation locally:

pip install finwise-python[docs]

Or with Nix:

nix develop .#docs
mkdocs serve

docs.title: Initialize the Client
docs.text:
from finwise import FinWise

# Using an API key directly
client = FinWise(api_key="your-api-key")

# Or use the FINWISE_API_KEY environment variable
client = FinWise()

docs.title: List Accounts
docs.text:
accounts = client.accounts.list()
for account in accounts:
    print(f"{account.name}: {account.currency} {account.balance}")

docs.title: Create a Transaction
docs.text:
from decimal import Decimal
from datetime import date

transaction = client.transactions.create(
    account_id="acc_123",
    amount=Decimal("-50.00"),
    transaction_date=date.today(),
    description="Grocery shopping",
    type="expense",
)

docs.title: Handle Errors
docs.text:
from finwise import FinWise, NotFoundError

client = FinWise()

try:
    account = client.accounts.retrieve("invalid_id")
except NotFoundError as e:
    print(f"Account not found: {e.message}")

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Configuration - Learn about client configuration options
  • Accounts - Full account management guide
  • Transactions - Working with transactions

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
Quick reference for all available methods.

For the official FinWise API documentation, see finwiseapp.io/docs/api.

docs.title: Client
docs.text: Method Description FinWise(api_key, ...) Create a new client client.close() Close the client

docs.title: Accounts
docs.text:
client.accounts

Method Description create(name, type, currency, ...) Create a new account retrieve(account_id) Get an account by ID update(account_id, ...) Update an account list(page_number, page_size) List all accounts archive(account_id) Archive an account

docs.title: Transactions
docs.text:
client.transactions

Method Description create(account_id, amount, transaction_date, ...) Create a transaction list(account_id, start_date, end_date, type, ...) List transactions aggregated(start_date, end_date, ...) Get aggregated summary archive(transaction_id) Archive a transaction

docs.title: Account Balances
docs.text:
client.account_balances

Method Description create(account_id, balance, balance_date) Create a balance record list(account_id, ...) List balance records aggregated(as_of_date, ...) Get aggregated balance archive(balance_id) Archive a balance record

docs.title: Transaction Categories
docs.text:
client.transaction_categories

Method Description create(name, color, icon, parent_id) Create a category list(parent_id, ...) List categories delete(category_id) Delete a category

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: The SDK provides a comprehensive exception hierarchy for handling errors.

docs.title: Exception Hierarchy
docs.text:
FinWiseError (base)
├── FinWiseAPIError (API returned an error)
│   ├── AuthenticationError (401)
│   ├── PermissionDeniedError (403)
│   ├── NotFoundError (404)
│   ├── ValidationError (400, 422)
│   ├── ConflictError (409)
│   ├── RateLimitError (429)
│   └── ServerError (5xx)
├── FinWiseConnectionError (connection failed)
└── FinWiseTimeoutError (request timed out)

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
from finwise import (
    FinWise,
    FinWiseError,
    FinWiseAPIError,
    AuthenticationError,
    PermissionDeniedError,
    NotFoundError,
    ValidationError,
    ConflictError,
    RateLimitError,
    ServerError,
    FinWiseConnectionError,
    FinWiseTimeoutError,
)

client = FinWise(api_key="your-api-key")

try:
    account = client.accounts.retrieve("invalid_id")
except NotFoundError as e:
    print(f"Not found: {e.message}")
    print(f"Request ID: {e.request_id}")  # For debugging
except AuthenticationError as e:
    print(f"Auth failed: {e.message}")
except RateLimitError as e:
    print(f"Rate limited. Retry after: {e.retry_after}s")
except FinWiseAPIError as e:
    print(f"API error {e.status_code}: {e.message}")
except FinWiseConnectionError as e:
    print(f"Connection failed: {e.message}")
except FinWiseError as e:
    print(f"Error: {e.message}")

docs.title: FinWiseAPIError
docs.text: Property Type Description message str Error message from the API status_code int HTTP status code request_id str Unique request ID for debugging

docs.title: RateLimitError
docs.text: Property Type Description retry_after int Seconds to wait before retrying

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Catch specific exceptions first, then broader ones
  2. Log the request_id when reporting issues to support
  3. Handle RateLimitError by waiting retry_after seconds
  4. Use FinWiseError as a catch-all for SDK errors

docs.title: Pagination
docs.text: All list endpoints support pagination and return a PaginatedResponse object.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
from finwise import FinWise

client = FinWise(api_key="your-api-key")

# Get first page of accounts
result = client.accounts.list(page_number=1, page_size=50)

print(f"Page {result.page_number} of {result.total_pages}")
print(f"Total items: {result.total_count}")

# Iterate through items on this page
for account in result.data:
    print(account.name)

# Access by index
first_account = result[0]

docs.title: Paginated Response
docs.text:
The PaginatedResponse[T] object contains:

Property Type Description data list[T] Items on the current page page_number int Current page number page_size int Items per page total_count int Total items across all pages total_pages int Total number of pages has_next bool Whether there's a next page has_previous bool Whether there's a previous page

docs.title: Iterating Through Pages
docs.text:
page_number = 1
all_transactions = []

while True:
    result = client.transactions.list(page_number=page_number, page_size=100)
    all_transactions.extend(result.data)

    if not result.has_next:
        break
    page_number += 1

print(f"Fetched {len(all_transactions)} total transactions")

docs.title: List Methods
docs.text:
All list methods return PaginatedResponse:

Method Return Type accounts.list() PaginatedResponse[Account] transactions.list() PaginatedResponse[Transaction] transaction_categories.list() PaginatedResponse[TransactionCategory] account_balances.list() PaginatedResponse[AccountBalance]

docs.title: Pagination Parameters
docs.text:
All list methods accept these parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page_number int 1 Page to retrieve (1-indexed) page_size int 100 Items per page

docs.title: Account Balances
docs.text: Track point-in-time balance snapshots for accounts.

docs.title: Create a Balance Record
docs.text:
from decimal import Decimal
from datetime import date
from finwise import FinWise

client = FinWise(api_key="your-api-key")

balance = client.account_balances.create(
    account_id="acc_123",
    balance=Decimal("5000.00"),
    balance_date=date(2024, 1, 15),
)

When to Use Balance Records

Balance records are useful for:

  • Tracking account balances over time
  • Reconciling with bank statements
  • Building net worth history

docs.title: List Balance Records
docs.text:
balances = client.account_balances.list()

for balance in balances:
    if balance.amount:
        print(f"{balance.date}: {balance.amount.format()}")

docs.title: Get Aggregated Balance History
docs.text:
Get balance history over time for a specific currency:

snapshots = client.account_balances.aggregated(currency="ZAR")

for snapshot in snapshots[-5:]:  # Last 5 snapshots
    print(f"{snapshot.date}: {snapshot.amount.format()}")

Currency Parameter

The currency parameter is required and specifies which currency to aggregate balances for (e.g., "USD", "EUR", "ZAR").

docs.title: Archive a Balance Record
docs.text: client.account_balances.archive("bal_123")

docs.title: Accounts
docs.text: Manage financial accounts (checking, savings, credit cards, etc.).

docs.title: Create an Account
docs.text:
from finwise import FinWise

client = FinWise(api_key="your-api-key")

account = client.accounts.create(
    name="Savings Account",
    type="savings",
    currency="USD",
    description="Emergency fund",
    initial_balance=10000.00,
)
print(f"Created: {account.id}")

docs.title: Account Types
docs.text: Type Description checking Checking/current account savings Savings account credit Credit card investment Investment/brokerage account loan Loan account other Other account type

docs.title: Retrieve an Account
docs.text:
account = client.accounts.retrieve("acc_123")
print(f"{account.name}: {account.balance}")

docs.title: Update an Account
docs.text:
account = client.accounts.update(
    "acc_123",
    name="Renamed Account",
    description="Updated description",
)

docs.title: List Accounts
docs.text:
accounts = client.accounts.list()
for account in accounts:
    print(f"  - {account.name}")

docs.title: Archive an Account
docs.text:
archived = client.accounts.archive("acc_123")
print(f"Archived at: {archived.archived_at}")

Note

Archiving is a soft delete. The account data is preserved but hidden from normal listings.

docs.title: Category Budgets
docs.text: Retrieve aggregated budget information by category.

docs.title: Get Aggregated Budgets
docs.text:
Get budget totals aggregated by category and transaction type:

from finwise import FinWise

client = FinWise(api_key="your-api-key")

budgets = client.category_budgets.aggregated(
    currency_code="ZAR",
)

for budget in budgets:
    print(f"Category {budget.category_id}: {budget.amount} ({budget.transaction_type})")

docs.title: With Date Filter
docs.text:
Filter budgets by budget period:

from datetime import datetime

budgets = client.category_budgets.aggregated(
    currency_code="ZAR",
    from_budget_period_to=datetime(2024, 3, 1),
)

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description currency_code str required Currency for aggregation (e.g., "ZAR", "USD") aggregate_by list[str] ["transactionCategoryId", "transactionType"] Fields to aggregate by from_budget_period_to datetime None End date for budget period filter

docs.title: Response Model
docs.text:
Each AggregatedCategoryBudget contains:

Field Type Description amount Decimal Budget amount for this category/type category_id str Transaction category ID transaction_type str Transaction type (e.g., "debit", "credit")

docs.title: Transaction Categories
docs.text: Organize transactions with categories and subcategories.

docs.title: Create a Category
docs.text:
from finwise import FinWise

client = FinWise(api_key="your-api-key")

category = client.transaction_categories.create(
    name="Groceries",
    color="#4CAF50",
    icon="shopping_cart",
)

docs.title: Create a Subcategory
docs.text:
subcategory = client.transaction_categories.create(
    name="Organic Food",
    color="#8BC34A",
    parent_id=category.id,
)

docs.title: List Categories
docs.text:
categories = client.transaction_categories.list()

for cat in categories:
    prefix = "  " if cat.is_subcategory else ""
    print(f"{prefix}{cat.name}")

docs.title: Delete a Category
docs.text:
client.transaction_categories.delete("cat_123")

Warning

Deleting a category will remove it permanently. Transactions using this category will have their category reference cleared.

docs.title: Transactions
docs.text: Record and query financial transactions.

docs.title: Create a Transaction
docs.text:
from decimal import Decimal
from datetime import date
from finwise import FinWise

client = FinWise(api_key="your-api-key")

transaction = client.transactions.create(
    account_id="acc_123",
    amount=Decimal("-50.00"),  # Negative for expenses
    transaction_date=date.today(),
    description="Grocery shopping",
    category_id="cat_groceries",
    type="expense",
)

docs.title: Transaction Types
docs.text:
Type Description income Money coming in expense Money going out transfer Transfer between accounts

Amount Sign Convention

Use negative amounts for expenses and positive amounts for income. This makes calculations straightforward.

docs.title: List Transactions
docs.text:
transactions = client.transactions.list()

for txn in transactions.data:
    print(f"{txn.transaction_date}: {txn.description} ({txn.amount.format()})")

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text:
Filter transactions by date range, type, or category:

from datetime import datetime

# Filter by date range
transactions = client.transactions.list(
    from_date=datetime(2024, 1, 1),
    to_date=datetime(2024, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59),
)

# Filter by transaction type
debits = client.transactions.list(transaction_types=["debit"])

# Filter by category
groceries = client.transactions.list(category_ids=["cat_groceries"])

# Combine filters
filtered = client.transactions.list(
    from_date=datetime(2024, 1, 1),
    transaction_types=["debit"],
    exclude_transfers=True,
)

docs.title: Filter Options
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description from_date datetime None Filter transactions from this date to_date datetime None Filter transactions up to this date transaction_types list[str] None Filter by types (e.g., ["debit", "credit"]) category_ids list[str] None Filter by category IDs exclude_archived bool True Exclude archived transactions exclude_transfers bool False Exclude transfer transactions exclude_excluded_transactions bool True Exclude manually excluded transactions exclude_parent_transactions bool True Exclude parent/split transactions

docs.title: Pagination
docs.text:
# Get first page
page1 = client.transactions.list(page_number=1, page_size=50)

print(f"Page {page1.page_number} of {page1.total_pages}")
print(f"Total transactions: {page1.total_count}")

# Get next page if available
if page1.has_next:
    page2 = client.transactions.list(page_number=2, page_size=50)

docs.title: Get Aggregated Totals
docs.text:
Get transaction totals aggregated by category and type:

# Get spending by category
aggregated = client.transactions.aggregated(
    currency_code="ZAR",
    time_zone="Africa/Johannesburg",
)

for item in aggregated:
    print(f"Category {item.category_id}: {item.amount} ({item.transaction_type})")

docs.title: Aggregation Options
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description currency_code str required Currency for aggregation (e.g., "ZAR", "USD") time_zone str "UTC" Time zone for date calculations aggregate_by list[str] ["transactionCategoryId", "transactionType"] Fields to aggregate by budget_start_date int 1 Day of month when budget period starts to_date datetime None End date for aggregation period exclude_archived bool True Exclude archived transactions exclude_transfers bool True Exclude transfer transactions exclude_excluded_transactions bool True Exclude manually excluded transactions exclude_parent_transactions bool True Exclude parent/split transactions

docs.title: Archive a Transaction
docs.text: client.transactions.archive("txn_123")
